title: Namespace infrastructure — multi-registry support on the Scheduler
purpose: Aion runs multiple versions of the same workflow simultaneously — workflow A on v1 code, workflow B on v2 code. Real OTP limits modules to two versions. Aion currently works around this with content-hash bytecode rewriting (renaming every module to name$hash), which is fragile and breaks closure module references. This brief introduces per-namespace module registries so that each namespace has its own module lookup space, eliminating the need for bytecode rewriting. The atom table, BIF registry, scheduler threads, and process table remain shared.
task: Add a NamespaceId type and a namespace store (HashMap<NamespaceId, Arc<ModuleRegistry>>) to the Scheduler's SharedState. Provide API methods to create namespaces, load modules into a specific namespace, and spawn processes scoped to a namespace. A default namespace preserves backwards compatibility — existing API calls operate on it. Scope: scheduler infrastructure and public API only. Process-scoped module lookup wiring is B-054.

requirements.title: NamespaceId type and NamespaceStore
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a NamespaceId type (newtype over u64) and a NamespaceStore (DashMap<NamespaceId, Arc<ModuleRegistry>>) on SharedState. THE SYSTEM SHALL pre-create a DEFAULT_NAMESPACE (NamespaceId(0)) at Scheduler construction, backed by the existing module_registry.
acceptance: NamespaceId is a #[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)] newtype over u64
NamespaceId::DEFAULT is a const for the default namespace (value 0)
SharedState gains namespace_store: DashMap<NamespaceId, Arc<ModuleRegistry>>
At Scheduler construction, namespace_store contains DEFAULT_NAMESPACE → Arc::clone of the shared module_registry
The existing module_registry field on SharedState is unchanged — it IS the default namespace's registry

requirements.title: Scheduler::create_namespace API
requirements.spec: WHEN Scheduler::create_namespace() is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL create a new empty ModuleRegistry, assign it a unique NamespaceId, store it in the namespace_store, and return the NamespaceId.
acceptance: create_namespace() -> NamespaceId allocates a new ID via an AtomicU64 counter
The new namespace starts with an empty ModuleRegistry
Multiple calls return distinct NamespaceIds
The namespace is immediately usable for load_module_in and spawn_in

requirements.title: Scheduler::load_module_in API
requirements.spec: WHEN Scheduler::load_module_in(namespace, bytes) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL load the module into the specified namespace's ModuleRegistry. Import resolution SHALL check the namespace's registry for cross-module references, falling back to the shared BIF registry. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT resolve imports against the default namespace or other namespaces.
acceptance: load_module_in(namespace: NamespaceId, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<HotLoadResult, LoadError>
The module is stored in the namespace's registry, not the default registry
Cross-module imports resolve against the namespace's registry (deferred resolution)
BIF imports resolve against the shared BIF registry (unchanged)
Loading the same module name in two different namespaces creates independent entries
Returns LoadError if the namespace doesn't exist

requirements.title: Scheduler::spawn_in API
requirements.spec: WHEN Scheduler::spawn_in(namespace, module, function, args) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the entry point from the namespace's registry and tag the spawned process with the namespace. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT resolve the entry point from the default namespace.
acceptance: spawn_in(namespace: NamespaceId, module: Atom, function: Atom, args: Vec<Term>) -> Result<u64, ExecError>
The entry module is looked up in the namespace's registry
The spawned process carries the NamespaceId (stored on the Process or in a per-PID map)
spawn_in_trap_exit variant also available for Aion's use case
Returns ExecError::Undef if the module is not in the namespace's registry

requirements.title: Process carries NamespaceId
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add a namespace_id: NamespaceId field to Process. WHEN a process is spawned via spawn_in, THE SYSTEM SHALL set its namespace_id to the specified namespace. WHEN a process is spawned via the existing spawn API, THE SYSTEM SHALL set its namespace_id to DEFAULT_NAMESPACE.
acceptance: Process gains namespace_id: NamespaceId field, defaulting to NamespaceId::DEFAULT
Process::namespace_id() -> NamespaceId accessor
spawn_in sets the process's namespace_id before making it runnable
Existing spawn/spawn_trap_exit/spawn_link use DEFAULT_NAMESPACE (backwards compatible)

requirements.title: Backwards-compatible existing API
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve all existing Scheduler methods unchanged. spawn, hot_load_module, purge_module, and force_purge_module SHALL operate on the default namespace. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require existing embedders to change any code.
acceptance: spawn(module, function, args) is equivalent to spawn_in(DEFAULT_NAMESPACE, module, function, args)
hot_load_module(bytes) loads into the default namespace
purge_module(name) and force_purge_module(name) operate on the default namespace
All existing tests pass without modification
beamr-cli works unchanged

requirements.title: Namespace-scoped hot code loading and purge
requirements.spec: WHEN hot_load_module_in(namespace, bytes) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL perform the dual-version load within the namespace's registry. WHEN purge_module_in(namespace, name) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL purge the old version from the namespace's registry only.
acceptance: hot_load_module_in(namespace: NamespaceId, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<HotLoadResult, LoadError>
purge_module_in(namespace: NamespaceId, name: Atom) -> Result<(), PurgeError>
force_purge_module_in(namespace: NamespaceId, name: Atom) -> Result<PurgeResult, PurgeError>
force_purge only kills processes in the specified namespace (checks process namespace_id)
Hot load within namespace A does not affect namespace B's view of the same module name

requirements.title: Tests for namespace infrastructure
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests verifying namespace creation, module isolation, and backwards compatibility.
acceptance: Test: create two namespaces, load same-named module with different code into each — lookup in ns1 returns v1, lookup in ns2 returns v2
Test: spawn_in(ns1, module, ...) runs v1 code, spawn_in(ns2, module, ...) runs v2 code
Test: default namespace spawn works identically to existing spawn API
Test: hot_load_module_in(ns1, ...) does not affect ns2
Test: force_purge_module_in(ns1, name) only kills ns1 processes
All existing tests pass unchanged

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement per-namespace atom tables — atom interning remains global and shared
SHALL NOT implement per-namespace BIF registries — BIFs are shared across all namespaces
SHALL NOT implement per-namespace process tables or PID spaces — processes are globally addressable
SHALL NOT implement cross-namespace module resolution — each namespace is isolated
SHALL NOT change the Scheduler constructor signature — namespace support is opt-in via create_namespace
verification: cargo test --workspace passes with all existing tests plus new namespace tests
Existing spawn/hot_load_module API calls work unchanged (backwards compatible)
Two namespaces with same-named module see different code
force_purge_module_in only affects the target namespace's processes
